caveats: MODERATE confidence — deterministic computation confirmed but some conditions pending activation (Stream 75.1)

value.narrative: Dependency Load: 68% of Architectural Relationships Are Dependency Edges
value.raw: Dependency Load: 68% of Architectural Relationships Are Dependency Edges

explanation: The program-level dependency load is computed at 0.682 — 15 of 22 architectural relationship edges are dependency relationships. This indicates a structurally dense dependency graph where most inter-component connections are direct load-bearing dependencies rather than loose event-driven couplings. R-038 confirms all backend modules DEPENDS_ON FleetEventsModule, making it the dependency hub.
transformation_summary: structural edge count → dependency edge count / total edge count = 0.682 (15/22)

signal.title: Dependency Load: 68% of Architectural Relationships Are Dependency Edges
signal.business_impact: A dependency load of 0.682 means most architectural connections are direct dependencies, elevating the blast radius of any component-level failure or change; deployment safety, change management, and incident containment all require accounting for this coupling density.
signal.risk: If any of the 15 dependency edges involves a WEAKLY GROUNDED component (such as Apache Kafka or Apache Flink) or an undocumented runtime dependency, the actual coupling may be higher than observed; unmanaged dependency concentration will increase non-linearly as new modules are added to the 63-module backend.
